implementation_difficulty_reason: Existing Delta F = 2 Wilson basis and kaon matrix-element machinery cover a conservative Delta m_K magnitude constraint; no new operator is required. Precision SM-subtracted treatment remains long-distance-theory-limited.

rg -n "Delta m_K|Delta M_K|Delta_m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|K_L|K_S|neutral-kaon|kaon mass difference|K neutral-kaon|non-CP kaon|_KAON_DELTA_M_K" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: docs/quark_scan_methodology_note.tex:118 lists Delta F = 2 neutral-meson observables, including Delta m_K.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:615 defines DELTA_M_K as the PDG K_L-K_S mass difference.
quarkConstraints/modern/inputs.py:958 defines the K mixing (Delta m_K) display name.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1329 routes system_id K through the Delta m_K bridge evaluator.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209 legacy DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS_V1 includes epsilon_K but not a standalone Delta m_K kaon input.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\Delta m_K \equiv m_{K_L}-m_{K_S}\).  This entry
covers the CP-even magnitude of neutral-kaon mixing, separate from the
CP-violating \(\varepsilon_K\) entry.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical PDG-sidecar value is the 2026 pdgLive fit assuming CPT
(\texttt{pdg\_live\_S013D\_20260516}),
\[
  \Delta m_K = (0.5293\pm0.0009)\times 10^{10}\,\hbar\,{\rm s}^{-1},
\]
with the quoted PDG uncertainty including a scale factor of \(1.3\).  The same
snapshot gives the companion fit not assuming CPT,
\((0.5289\pm0.0010)\times 10^{10}\,\hbar\,{\rm s}^{-1}\).  Both values trace
to \texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K002/pdg\_live\_S013D\_20260516.txt}.
sections.relevance:
In anarchic warped flavor models, tree-level KK-gluon exchange generates
\(\Delta S=2\) four-quark operators after rotating to the quark-mass basis.
\(\Delta m_K\) constrains the real part of the new \(K^0-\bar K^0\) mixing
amplitude, while \(\varepsilon_K\) constrains its imaginary part.  The same
VLL, VRR, SLL/SLR, and LR operator structures used for the repo's neutral-meson
mixing lane apply here, and the LR matrix elements remain chirally enhanced.
The important physics caveat is that the measured mass splitting contains a
large long-distance Standard Model component, so the catalog should treat
\(\Delta m_K\) as a conservative magnitude bound unless a controlled SM
subtraction is supplied.
sections.post_2008:
Relative to the CFW 2008 baseline (\texttt{cfw\_2008}), the experimental
side was updated by KTeV's final neutral-kaon analysis
(\texttt{ktev\_2011}), which the PDG fit still uses.  The more important
post-2008 shift is theoretical: lattice calculations began attacking the
long-distance contribution directly.  Christ et al.
(\texttt{christ\_2013\_ld\_delta\_mk}) developed the long-distance method,
and Bai et al. (\texttt{bai\_2014\_delta\_mk}) reported a first complete
lattice calculation,
\(\Delta M_K=3.19(41)(96)\times10^{-12}\,{\rm MeV}\), at
heavier-than-physical masses.  Later physical-quark-mass studies
(\texttt{wang\_2023\_delta\_mk}) reached statistical uncertainty near
\(9\%\) while retaining important systematics.  For BSM interpretation, FLAG
2024 (\texttt{flag2024}) and the physical-mass BSM kaon-mixing calculation
(\texttt{boyle\_2024\_bsm\_kaon\_mixing}) update the bag inputs used to
translate Wilson coefficients into matrix elements.
sections.validity:
The experimental number is precise, but \(\Delta m_K\) is long-distance
limited as a new-physics constraint.  A conservative RS scan can require the
absolute new-physics contribution not to exceed the measured splitting, or use
only a fraction of it by policy.  A sharper bound would require specifying the
short-distance charm-loop treatment, the long-distance lattice input, and the
CPT-fit convention.  This entry is therefore robust as a conservative
\(\Delta F=2\) magnitude check, but not as a clean SM-subtracted exclusion.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{PARTIAL.}  The methodology note lists \(\Delta m_K\) among the five
\(\Delta F=2\) scan observables at
\texttt{docs/quark\_scan\_methodology\_note.tex:118}.  The legacy kaon helper
sets the PDG mass splitting at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:615} and
implements \texttt{evaluate\_delta\_mk} at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:755}.  The modern lane also has a
\texttt{K} input displayed as \(\Delta m_K\) at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/inputs.py:958} and a bridge evaluator at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:493}.  I keep the status
partial because the legacy default kaon input is still the \(\varepsilon_K\)
entry at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209}, and the catalog has not
audited a standalone long-distance policy.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{LOW.}  No new operator basis is needed for a conservative catalog or
scan-side magnitude bound: the existing \(\Delta F=2\) VLL/VRR/LR machinery
covers the required Wilson coefficients.  A future precision SM-subtracted
\(\Delta m_K\) likelihood would be a separate high-theory-effort project.
